mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce borrowing costs for corporations and increase the present value of future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in the residential real estate sector, which amplifies consumer spending and financial sector leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive MoM growth
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge in direction, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and manufacturing output growth, which translates into short-term positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >65% of high-inflation months, or the negative relationship vanishes when inflation is driven by supply shocks rather than demand.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with stable or declining unemployment confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition triggers but SPX returns are negative in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which improves corporate revenue trajectories and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters with positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and falling capacity utilization, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of inventory drawdowns rather than demand shocks.
